hook: RT and Axios World — opposite political universes — both said the same thing about Hantavirus outbreak on MV Hondius. Nobody else did.

tweets.text: 33 buckets, 54 articles, 6 distinct frames in today's coverage.
Frame: HEALTH_SAFETY (20 buckets). "World Health Organisation (WHO) chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said on Tuesday “our work is not over” to contain hantavirus after evacuat…" — Dawn
Frame: CAPACITY_RESOURCES (5 buckets). "The last remaining passengers on a cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak disembarked Monday and boarded flights to more than 20 c…" — Globe and Mail
Frame: PUBLIC_OPINION (4 buckets). "For many people, news of a virus outbreak on a cruise ship immediately brings back memories of COVID spreading when the Ruby Princess docke…" — The Conversation

Frame: QUALITY_OF_LIFE (4 buckets). ""Losing my dad and my two sisters in less than a month..." she told AFP, trailing off.
Her voice broke and she laughed nervously, opting to…" — Buenos Aires Herald

The paradox: Russian state media (RT) and US journalism (Axios) independently ran 'not another COVID' reassurance frames within the same news cycle, converging on identical public-health messaging despite their structural geopolitical antagonism. (RT & Axios World, opposite bloc…

Silence as data: `china` didn't cover this — they covered No articles in this corpus. China's extensive SARS and COVID-19 experience would make it a natural commentator on ship-borne viral outbreaks; it appears to have prioritised Hormuz and Ukraine stories instead. instead.

**Le Figaro** (wire_services): Only outlet to run the patient-zero origin story: Dutch ornithologist couple visited an Argentine landfill to observe the rare white-throated Caracara before boarding MV Hondius.
